ocr.text: In recent studies focusing on genetic variation and its implications for phenotypic traits, the analysis of specific single
nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) has garnered significant attention. The data presented in the accompanying table summarizes
the additive genetic variance (σ²a) associated with five Q-Tag SNPs. This variance is critical for understanding the heritability
of traits influenced by these genetic markers. Specifically, the values for σ²a indicate a range of contributions from each Q-Tag
SNP, with SNP1 showing an additive genetic variance of 0.113, which is notably higher than that of the subsequent SNPs.
Haplotype based on
Q-Tag SNP1  2 Q-Tag SNP  3 Q-Tag SNP  4 Q-Tag SNP  5 Q-Tag SNP
Estimated effectb
SNP2, SNP3, and SNP4 exhibit variances of 0.082, 0.084, and 0.084 respectively, suggesting a relatively consistent influence
on the traits under investigation. These findings underscore the importance of each SNP in contributing to the overall genetic
architecture of the studied traits. Furthermore, the low variance associated with SNP5, which mirrors that of SNP3 and SNP4,
raises questions about its functional relevance and potential interactions with other genetic or environmental factors. Continued
exploration of these SNPs may provide deeper insights into their roles in genetic predisposition and trait expression.
